title: Deaths from fires and burns
subtitle: Estimated annual number of deaths due to fire, heat, and hot substances.
caption:
Deaths from fires and burns

Estimated annual number of deaths due to fire, heat, and hot substances.

This choropleth world map displays the estimated annual number of deaths due to fire, heat, and hot substances for the year 2023. The color scale ranges from light beige representing zero deaths to a deep maroon indicating 10,000 or more deaths, with intermediate thresholds at 100, 300, 1,000, and 3,000. India stands out as the only country falling into the darkest maroon band, signifying a death toll exceeding 10,000. The United States and Russia appear in a dark red shade that corresponds to the band between 3,000 and 10,000 deaths. A broad cluster of nations, including Brazil, China, and several countries across Sub-Saharan Africa such as Nigeria and the Democratic Republic of the Congo, sit in the mid-range orange bands, indicating values generally between 300 and 3,000. Much of Western Europe, Canada, and Australia are depicted in lighter beige and pale orange shades, placing them in the lower ranges of the scale, typically below 300 deaths. Greenland and a few small island territories are marked with diagonal hatching to indicate no data is available. The visual distribution highlights a significant concentration of higher mortality figures in South Asia and parts of Eastern Europe and North America, while many regions in Oceania and Northern Europe show the lowest recorded numbers.
